x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces OATH's open adjudication data (ECB summonses, hearings, decisions, Trials Division cases) and adds dispute_summons — the net-new write workflow locked today inside the legacy Apache Struts ECB Ticket Finder portal.

server.name: io.nyc.oath
server.title: NYC Administrative Trials & Hearings (OATH)
server.description: Agent-native access to the NYC Office of Administrative Trials & Hearings — look up ECB summonses (tickets), their charges, hearings, decisions, penalties, and balances, plus OATH Trials Division cases, from NYC Open Data; and the net-new ability to respond to / dispute a summons (request a hearing) that today lives only in the legacy ECB Ticket Finder portal.
server.instructions: Use find_summonses / get_summons to locate ECB summonses by ticket number, issuing agency, respondent, borough, status, or date, and get_summons_hearing / get_summons_decision to drill into the hearing and disposition. find_trial_cases / get_trial_case cover the OATH Trials Division. dispute_summons (net-new) submits a response to a summons — dispute it, request or reschedule a hearing, submit a defense, or reopen a default; it requires an authenticated respondent or authorized representative and mutates state, so confirm the ticket number, request type, and defense with the user before submitting. list_my_disputes / get_dispute_status read the respondent's own requests. OATH adjudicates but does not issue summonses; note that penalties and docketed judgments are ultimately collected by the Department of Finance.

tools.name: find_summonses
tools.title: Find Summonses
tools.description: Search ECB summonses (tickets) adjudicated by OATH by ticket number, issuing agency, respondent, borough, hearing status, and/or date range.

tools.name: get_summons
tools.title: Get Summons
tools.description: Fetch a single ECB summons (charges, respondent, violation location, penalty, balance) by ticket number.

tools.name: get_summons_hearing
tools.title: Get Summons Hearing
tools.description: Get the scheduled hearing (date, time, location, result) for a summons.

tools.name: get_summons_decision
tools.title: Get Summons Decision
tools.description: Get the decision / disposition (hearing result, penalty imposed, docketed judgment, balance due) for a summons.

tools.name: find_trial_cases
tools.title: Find Trial Cases
tools.description: Search OATH Trials Division cases by type, category, filing agency case ID, or free text.

tools.name: get_trial_case
tools.title: Get Trial Case
tools.description: Fetch a single OATH Trials Division case by case number.

tools.name: list_my_disputes
tools.title: List My Disputes
tools.description: List the authenticated respondent's own summons disputes / hearing requests and their status — today visible only inside the ECB Ticket Finder portal.

tools.name: get_dispute_status
tools.title: Get Dispute Status
tools.description: Get the status of a submitted summons dispute by dispute/confirmation number.

tools.name: dispute_summons
tools.title: Dispute a Summons
tools.description: Respond to an ECB summons — dispute it, request or reschedule a hearing, submit a written defense, or reopen a default. NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — it lives only in the legacy Apache Struts ECB Ticket Finder portal or by mail/in person. Requires an authenticated respondent or authorized representative. Mutates state — confirm the ticket number, request type, and defense first.

x-agent-skills.note: Each tool maps to one of the ten NYC common government-process Agent Skills via x-agent-skill.

prompts.name: explore_oath
prompts.description: Search and read NYC Admin Trials & Hearings (OATH) records — Summonses, Hearings, Decisions, Trials.

prompts.name: dispute_oath
prompts.description: SummonsDispute — respond to / dispute a summons (request a hearing) via an owned API

prompts.name: check_oath_status
prompts.description: Check the status of a NYC Admin Trials & Hearings (OATH) application, case, or request.

resources.name: NYC Admin Trials & Hearings (OATH) — profile
resources.description: NYC Admin Trials & Hearings (OATH): 9 operations, 9 tools, verb “respond”.

resources.name: Summonses record (templated)
resources.description: A single NYC Admin Trials & Hearings (OATH) Summonses record by id.
